However, if you are referring to "Ursinia" or "Ursinia caffra" (commonly known as "Ursifolium" in some texts) - it is a type of flowering plant belonging to the daisy family. Ursinia, or "Ursifolium," has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, and the most common herbal preparations made from it include teas and infusions, as well as capsules or tinctures.
